mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate transmits through a lower discount rate and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when policy is moving down from restrictive levels.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative or below baseline in more than 60% of months where FEDFUNDS is declining for at least 2 consecutive observations, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is still elevated and the policy rate is above 4.5%, equity multiples compress as investors price persistent real-rate pressure, so the S&P 500 should underperform over the next 20 trading days after such threshold states appear.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation remains above 3% and FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate indicates weakening household income and demand, which should precede lower corporate revenue expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of episodes after the unemployment trigger, or if the sign of the effect reverses in late-cycle windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth reflects resilient consumer demand and supports near-term earnings expectations, so the S&P 500 should rise over the next week when sales surprise on the upside.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS is above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of above-trend retail sales observations, or if the signal’s short-horizon Rank IC is persistently near zero.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production improves while housing starts do not deteriorate, the market reads broad real-activity momentum without an obvious recessionary shock, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform single-series effects and if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in more than 55% of episodes where INDPRO rises while HOUST is flat or rising.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts signals weaker interest-sensitive demand and a cooling business cycle, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days when starts break below recent trend.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of below-trend housing starts episodes, or if the signal does not weaken once rates are stable.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with a low unemployment rate indicates sustained labor-income support and less recession risk, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises month over month while UNRATE is at or below 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered labor-strength observations are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the combined condition is not stronger than PAYEMS or UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production during a moderate inflation environment signals real output growth without immediate overheating, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in more than 55% of periods where INDPRO is rising and CPIAUCSL inflation stays below 4%, or if the effect vanishes when inflation is stable.
